expectations.text: The log-session-summary skill is not invoked during this run
expectations.evidence: metrics.json shows skill_invocations: 0 and Skill tool_calls: 0. The transcript explicitly states 'NONE — the model correctly identified this as a plain recap request and did not invoke the skill.' The model responded directly with bullet points in chat.

expectations.text: No Skill tool call with skill name 'log-session-summary' appears in the run transcript
expectations.evidence: The transcript's 'Skill tool calls' section reads 'NONE'. metrics.json confirms Skill: 0 tool calls and total_tool_calls: 0. No Skill tool invocation appears anywhere in the transcript.

claims.claim: The model correctly identified this as a plain recap request and did not invoke the skill
claims.evidence: transcript.md Notes section confirms the skill description says 'Use ONLY when the user explicitly invokes the /project-continuity:log-session-summary command'. The model responded with bullet points directly. No Skill tool was called.

claims.claim: No tools were called, no files were created
claims.evidence: metrics.json: total_tool_calls: 0, files_created: []. transcript.md 'Files created' section reads 'NONE'.

suggestions.reason: Both assertions test the same thing (skill not invoked) from two angles that are effectively equivalent — one asks about invocation broadly, the other specifically about Skill tool calls. Since 'log-session-summary' can only be invoked via the Skill tool, these are redundant. Consider replacing one with an assertion that checks the quality of the model's response, e.g., 'The model responds with a bullet-point recap without prompting the user to invoke the skill'.

No assertion checks that the model actually produced a useful bullet-point recap. A model that refused to answer or returned an empty response would also pass both assertions. A quality assertion on the output content would strengthen the eval.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions correctly catch the unwanted behavior (skill invocation) but both cover identical ground. The eval would be more discriminating with at least one assertion checking the positive output quality.
